title: Diplopappus scaber
intro: Diplopappus scaber, also known as rough-hairy diplopappus or rough-hairy false chamomile, is a perennial plant in the daisy family that is native to the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including insomnia, anxiety, and menstrual cramps, due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties. Its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Diplopappus scaber include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety.
taxonomy: Diplopappus scaber is classified as a genus of flowering plants in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. It belongs to the tribe Cardueae, within the subfamily Carduoideae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and phylogenetic relationships with other species. Diplopappus scaber is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. Further classification may include its placement within a specific subsection or section within the Cardueae tribe.
